name: Michael Philip Mossman
abstract: Michael Philip Mossman (born October 12, 1959) is an American jazz trumpeter.

concerts.label: JVC Jazz Festival: A Gala Tribute to Celia Cruz and Tito Puente
JVC Jazz Festival: Re-Birth of the Cool
JVC Jazz Festival: Havana-New York Jam/ The Two Worlds of Ray Barretto
concerts.venue: Isaac Stern Auditorium / Ronald O. Perelman Stage

about_quotes.speaker: Harry Allen
about_quotes.evidence: I see so many of the people that were there during my four years at Rutgers, on the road. David Sanchez was there, Michael Mossman, Ralph Bowen, Steve Nelson, and there was a bunch of others.
about_quotes.doc_title: Harry Allen interviewed by Monk Rowe, Chautauqua, New York, September 13, 1997

about_quotes.speaker: Jerome Richardson
about_quotes.evidence: There are very fine arrangements, especially by Mike Mossman... But the way they played them, the way we played the music
about_quotes.doc_title: Jerome Richardson interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, March 9, 1996

about_quotes.speaker: Jimmy Heath
about_quotes.evidence: I was instrumental in getting Michael Mossman; he’s the head of the program now.
about_quotes.doc_title: Jimmy Heath — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Kenny Barron
about_quotes.evidence: Yeah. Mike is teaching now at, I think, Queens College, where Jimmy Heath was.
about_quotes.doc_title: Kenny Barron — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
